**Balatro**

Balatro won multiple Game of the Year honors at the 2025 Game Developers Choice Awards. It takes poker hand mechanics and wraps them in a roguelike deck-building system filled with Joker cards and absurd synergies.
 
Every run plays differently, and the offline mode is complete and uninterrupted.

Slay the Spire is universally regarded as one of the best games to come out of the roguelike card-game genre. Each run you choose a character, build a deck from scratch, and battle through a tower of increasingly dangerous enemies. The combination options are near-infinite, and the strategic depth is exceptional.

blind man's bluff card game app for Andr.response:
If you are looking to play **Blind Man's Bluff** (also widely known as **Indian Poker** or **Indian Head**) on Android, several real, existing apps on the Google Play Store bring this classic card-on-forehead game to your mobile device:

### 1. Blindex - Blind Card Game
* **Overview:** This is a dedicated, single-player offline version of Blind Man's Bluff. 
* **How it works:** The app simulates the classic gameplay where you "hold" your card to your head. Since you can only see your opponents' cards, you must read the AI's behavioral "tells" and decide whether to fold, call, or raise across three different difficulty levels. It is a great option if you want to practice your bluffing strategy solo without needing an internet connection.

### 2. Indian Poker - Card Game (by Wooah Tech Inc.)
* **Overview:** A multiplayer-focused adaptation of the game designed to be played with friends and family.
* **How it works:** True to the rules of Blind Man's Bluff, you predict your victory by looking only at your opponent's card. The app emphasizes psychological warfare, encouraging you to read your opponents' expressions and master your own tells to win the round. 

### 3. Indian poker (by Kanbe)
* **Overview:** A straightforward, no-frills simulation of the classic card game.
* **How it works:** This app is designed to act as a digital deck for multiple people playing together in the same room. It simplifies the setup of physical cards, allowing a group of players to use their devices to display their cards to everyone else while keeping their own hand a mystery to themselves. 

### 4. Teen Patti Gold (by Moonfrog)
* **Overview:** While *Teen Patti* is technically a three-card brag game, it is widely referred to and played as "Indian Poker" throughout the Google Play Store. 
* **How it works:** If you are looking for a massive online multiplayer experience with similar bluffing mechanics, this app features "pot-blind" variations. You can play with millions of players worldwide or set up private tables to play with friends.

forehead poker app Android.response:
Depending on whether you are looking for the **classic card game** (where you hold a playing card to your forehead without looking at it) or the **party word-guessing game** (where you hold your phone to your forehead and guess words based on clues), there are several excellent Android apps available on the Google Play Store:

### 1. Classic "Forehead Poker" (Indian Poker / Blind Man's Bluff)
In these games, you see everyone else's cards but your own, and you must bet or bluff based on what you see on their "foreheads."

*   **Indian Poker - Card Game** (by Wooah Tech Inc.): This app is dedicated entirely to the classic psychological card game. It allows you to play with friends and family, focusing on reading your opponents' expressions and betting strategically based only on the cards you can see on their screens.
*   **Indian poker** (by Kanbe): A simple, straightforward simulation of the traditional card-on-forehead game. It is designed for local multiplayer play using virtual playing cards.
*   **Anipang Poker / 애니팡 포커** (by Playlinks): If you are looking for a more polished, casino-style package, this popular multiplayer poker app features "Indian Poker" as one of its primary playable game modes alongside traditional poker variants.

---

### 2. "Forehead" Party & Word Guessing Games (Heads Up! Style)
If you meant the popular party game genre where you hold the phone to your forehead and guess words, phrases, or celebrities based on your friends' clues, these are the top Android options:

*   **Heads Up!** (by Warner Bros. International Enterprises): The official, wildly popular game popularized by Ellen DeGeneres. You place the phone on your forehead, and your friends shout clues to help you guess the word on the screen before the timer runs out. It features themed decks ranging from blockbuster movies to animals and pop culture.
*   **Charades & Headbands: Guess Up** (by Cosmicode): A highly-rated alternative to Heads Up! that features team modes, custom deck creation, and the ability to record video of your friends acting out clues so you can watch the hilarious footage later.
*   **ForeHeads: Charades Guess Game**: A clean, ad-free party game app featuring over 48 unique decks and thousands of challenges. It is built specifically for group game nights and family gatherings.

card game where you can't see your own c.response:
If you are looking for Android apps where you cannot see your own card (a mechanic often found in games like **Indian Poker**, **Blind Man's Bluff**, or cooperative games like **Hanabi**), here are the best options available on the Google Play Store:

### 1. **Blindex — Blind Man's Bluff** (by Blindex)
* **Description:** This is a dedicated, single-player offline version of Blind Man's Bluff (also known as Indian Poker). In this game, you go head-to-head against an AI dealer. You are dealt a card that is visible to the dealer but hidden from you, while you can see the dealer's card. You must use psychology, betting strategy, and bluffing to win the round without ever seeing your own hand. 

### 2. **Indian Poker - Card Game** (by Wooah Tech Inc.)
* **Description:** If you want to play the classic "forehead poker" game with friends and family, this app is designed for local multiplayer. Each player's card is displayed on the screen for others to see, but remains hidden from the cardholder. You have to read your opponents' facial expressions and betting patterns to guess whether your hidden card is higher or lower than theirs.

### 3. **Heads Up!** (by Warner Bros. International Enterprises)
* **Description:** While technically a word-guessing party game rather than a traditional playing-card game, *Heads Up!* is the most famous digital implementation of the "card on the forehead" mechanic. You hold your phone up to your forehead with the screen facing your friends. A "card" with a word, celebrity, or phrase appears on the screen. You cannot see it, and your friends must shout clues or act it out to help you guess what card you are holding before the timer runs out.

### 4. **Board Game Arena** (Playable via Android Browser)
* **Description:** If you are looking for **Hanabi**—the ultimate cooperative card game where you can see everyone's cards *except* your own—the best way to play it on Android is through the Board Game Arena platform. In Hanabi, players must work together to launch a spectacular fireworks display by playing cards in a specific numerical sequence. Because you cannot see your own hand, you must rely entirely on the highly restricted, tactical clues your teammates give you to figure out what you are holding.

The type of game you are looking for is a digital version of "charades" or "headbands," where the phone acts as the card. You hold the screen to your forehead facing your friends, and they act out or describe the word on the screen for you to guess.

### 1. **Heads Up!** (by Warner Bros. International Enterprises)
This is the official, incredibly popular game popularized on *The Ellen DeGeneres Show*. 
* **How it works:** You select a category (like Blockbuster Movies, Animals, Accents, or Celebrities), hold the phone to your forehead, and try to guess the words based on your friends' clues. 
* **Controls:** Tilt the phone down when you guess correctly, or tilt it up to skip.
* **Bonus Feature:** It can use your phone's front camera to record hilarious videos of your friends shouting clues so you can watch them later.

### 2. **Charades!** (by FatChicken Studios)
A massive, highly-rated free alternative to *Heads Up!*
* **How it works:** It features a clean, colorful interface with dozens of diverse categories ranging from pop culture to science. 
* **Modes:** It includes a "Versus" mode, allowing you to split your party into teams and keep score over multiple rounds.

### 3. **Charades & Headbands: Guess Up** (by Cosmicode)

* **Features:** It supports multiple languages, allows you to record your gameplay, and even lets you **create your own custom categories** to play with friends.

### 4. **ForeHeads: Charades Guess Game**
Another excellent "headbands" style app designed for family game nights and parties.
* **How it works:** It features over 48 unique decks and thousands of challenges. 
* **Features:** It is designed to be completely ad-free during gameplay so your party isn't interrupted by annoying pop-ups.

single player bluffing card game Android.response:
Here are some of the best real, offline, single-player bluffing card games available on the Google Play Store:

### 1. **Bluff Card Game** (by Vertex Games)
* **Overview:** This is a highly polished offline adaptation of the classic bluffing card game widely known as **Cheat**, **I Doubt It**, or **BS**. 
* **Offline & Single-Player Features:** The game is fully playable without an internet connection or WiFi. It features a **2-Player Mode** for quick 1v1 matches against a smart AI, as well as a **4-Player Mode** for a full-table experience against three AI opponents. It also includes daily quests, profile statistics tracking, and adjustable AI difficulty.

### 2. **Cheat! – Bluffing Card Game**
* **Overview:** A dedicated single-player card game designed specifically around the mechanics of "Cheat" and reading your opponents.
* **Offline & Single-Player Features:** You play offline in intense 4-player matches against three CPU opponents. The game features a unique "title system" that labels you as a *Liar*, *Honest*, or *Hunter* based on your playstyle. It also introduces strategic power-up items like Lie Counters and Bluff Shields to add extra depth to the offline gameplay.

### 3. **Cleverfox - I Doubt It**
* **Overview:** An excellent adaptation of the classic card game of deception, supporting many international rule variations of the game (such as *Verish' Ne Verish'*, *Mogeln*, or *Russian Bluff*).
* **Offline & Single-Player Features:** It allows you to challenge computer-controlled opponents entirely offline. You can customize the gameplay by choosing different rule variations (such as playing cards of the same rank, lower cards, upper cards, or random sequences) and unlock different card designs and themes.

### 4. **Liar's Dice** (by YOAMB B.V.) — *Bonus Dice Option*
* **Overview:** If you are open to bluffing games outside of traditional playing cards, this is the definitive mobile version of the classic bluffing dice game (also known as *Perudo* or *Dudo*).
* **Offline & Single-Player Features:** While it has a major online multiplayer component, it features a robust **Offline Practice Mode**. You can play offline against highly intelligent AI opponents to sharpen your bidding and bluffing skills without needing an internet connection.

best offline single player card game And.response:
Here are some of the best offline, single-player card games available on the Google Play Store for Android:

### 1. **Balatro**
* **Genre:** Roguelike Poker Deckbuilder
* **Why it’s great:** *Balatro* is a massive hit that brings a hypnotic, poker-themed deck-building experience to mobile. Your goal is to play illegal poker hands to score chips and defeat "blinds". The real magic comes from collecting "Joker" cards, which trigger wild, game-altering multipliers and synergies. It is incredibly addictive, runs flawlessly offline, and has been fully optimized for touchscreens.

### 2. **Slay the Spire**
* **Genre:** Roguelike Deckbuilder
* **Why it’s great:** Widely considered the gold standard of the roguelike deck-building genre, *Slay the Spire* tasks you with climbing a procedurally generated tower. You choose from four distinct characters, each with their own unique card pool and playstyles. As you defeat enemies, you draft new cards and collect powerful relics to build unstoppable synergies. It is a premium game with zero ads or microtransactions, making it perfect for offline play.

### 3. **Dawncaster: Deckbuilding RPG**
* **Genre:** Dark Fantasy RPG Deckbuilder
* **Why it’s great:** Designed specifically for mobile, *Dawncaster* is played in portrait mode, making it incredibly comfortable to play with one hand. It features over 900 hand-illustrated cards and multiple classes (like Rogue, Warrior, and Arcanist). It plays like a hybrid of a choose-your-own-adventure RPG and a deep deckbuilder. It is a premium game that gives you access to all cards without paywalls or internet requirements.

### 4. **Pirates Outlaws**
* **Genre:** Pirate-themed Roguelike Card Game
* **Why it’s great:** If you enjoy *Slay the Spire* but want a different aesthetic, *Pirates Outlaws* is a fantastic alternative. You recruit a crew, manage your "ammo" (which acts as your action points), and navigate through various maps to defeat outlaw bosses. It features a massive roster of playable characters, each with their own pre-made decks and special mechanics, and works entirely offline.

### 5. **Card Thief**
* **Genre:** Stealth Puzzle Card Game
* **Why it’s great:** *Card Thief* reimagines stealth gameplay as a solitaire-style card game. Played on a 3x3 grid of cards, you guide a thief card through a dungeon, avoiding guards, extinguishing torches, hiding in shadows, and stealing treasure. It is a highly tactical, slow-paced puzzle game that is perfect for quick, offline gaming sessions.

### 6. **Night of the Full Moon**
* **Genre:** Dark Fairy Tale Deckbuilder
* **Why it’s great:** Based on the story of Little Red Riding Hood, this game features a gorgeous, dark-gothic art style. You explore a mysterious forest, encountering various monsters and choosing how to build your deck based on your class (such as Ranger, Knight, or Nun). It is played in portrait mode, features a compelling story, and offers a highly polished offline single-player campaign.

blind man's bluff card game app for Andr.response:
The Android app for **Blind Man's Bluff** (also known as Indian Poker or Forehead Poker) is called **Blindex**.

**Blindex** is a free, offline single-player card game where you play head-to-head against an AI dealer [1]. Key features include:
*   **Game Mechanic**: You hold a card face-out against your forehead so you cannot see it, but the AI opponent can [1].
*   **Offline Play**: It runs fully offline with no internet connection required [1].
*   **Difficulty Levels**: You can choose from three difficulty levels, each with a different AI bluffing style and "tell" to read [1].
*   **Cost**: It is 100% free to download and play with no ads or in-app purchases [1].
*   **Availability**: It is available worldwide on Google Play for Android [1].

You can find it on Google Play by searching for "Blindex" or visiting the official site at `blindex.app` [1].

*Note: There is another app called **Bluff Online** on Google Play, but it is a different card game where the goal is to get rid of your cards by lying about their values, not the Blind Man's Bluff poker variant [2].*

forehead poker app Android.response:
There is no Android app called “forehead poker,” but you likely mean **Indian Poker** (the card-on-forehead game) or a **face-to-face/live video poker** app. For Indian Poker rules, see [10]. For Android apps that support live video or face-to-face poker with friends, try these real, existing apps on Google Play:

- **Texas Holdem Poker Face Live** – Online poker face games for beginners and pros; includes unlimited chips and a $50,000 welcome bonus [4].

If you specifically want to play Indian Poker (card-on-forehead), there’s no dedicated app, but you can play it manually using physical cards while using one of the above apps to track chip counts for pick-up games.

card game where you can't see your own c.response:
The card game you’re describing is likely **B.S.** (also known as “Bluff” or “I Doubt It”), where players’ cards are hidden from themselves unless someone calls “B.S.” to challenge a play[1].

For Android, the best app that lets you play this type of hidden-card bluffing game is:

- **Card Game Simulator** by Finol Digital LLC – This app lets you create, import, and play custom card games (including B.S./Bluff) with friends on a virtual tabletop[4]. You can set up rules where cards are hidden and only revealed when challenged.

If you want a ready-made app specifically for B.S./Bluff, search Google Play for:
- **“B.S. Card Game”** or **“Bluff Card Game”** – Several user-made apps exist with this exact mechanic (hidden cards, bluffing, and calling “B.S.”).

Let me know if you’d like links to specific Play Store entries.

Android card game where you hold card to.response:
The Android game you’re describing is **Heads Up!** (also known as **Heads Up! – The Forehead Game**), where one player holds their phone to their forehead with the screen facing out, and friends give clues to help them guess the word displayed on the screen [3][4][7].

Another similar option is the **Charades & Headbands Game** (also called **Charades & Headbands: Guess Up**), which follows the same forehead-holding mechanic but focuses on charades-style clues like dancing, singing, or miming [2][9].

If you’re looking for the physical card game version that uses a headband instead of a phone, it’s called **Hedbanz**, where players clip a picture card to a headband on their forehead and guess what it is by asking yes/no questions [1].

For Android, the most popular and widely used app is **Heads Up!** by Warner Bros. (Ellen DeGeneres), available on Google Play [4][8].

single player bluffing card game Android.response:
There is **no dedicated single-player bluffing card game** (like the traditional "Bluff" or "Open!" game) available on Android that is both **offline** and **single-player**, as the core mechanic of bluffing inherently requires opponents to call out lies.

However, based on your request for an **offline Android card game** with **bluffing/guessing strategy**, here are the best real alternatives available on Google Play Store:

| App Name | Why It Fits Your Request |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Card Thief** | A **single-player offline** puzzle card game where you must **guess** and **bluff** your way through levels by monitoring enemy behavior and calling their moves. It is highly strategic and entirely offline [6]. |
| **Spades - Offline Card Games** | A classic **single-player offline** card game with high-level AI. While not "Bluff" specifically, it involves **monitoring opponents' behavior** and strategic guessing, similar to bluffing mechanics [5]. |
| **Night of the Full Moon** | A **single-player offline** roguelite deck-builder where you must **guess** enemy patterns and bluff your way through battles. It is free to try and fully playable offline without ads [2]. |
| **Card Quest** | A **single-player offline** deck-building roguelite where strategy and **guessing** enemy moves are key. It is recommended for offline single-player card fans [2]. |

**Key Clarification:**
The traditional game "Bluff" (where you place cards face-down and declare a rank, then opponents call "Open!") is almost exclusively a **multiplayer** game. The app "Bluff - Cards Game" on Google Play is primarily a **multiplayer online** game with chat features, not a single-player offline version [1][4]. If you specifically need the traditional "Bluff" rules, you must play with friends online or locally.

For a true **single-player offline** experience with bluffing/guessing strategy, **Card Thief** is the most precise recommendation [6].
